title: Nonlinear softening mechanism during inoculated fermentation of Zaoyu based on histology and 4D-DIA proteomics.
author_string: Nie, Jinggui, Zhang, Xiao, Wang, Yong, Gao, Yumeng, Huang, Qilin
abstract: Inoculated fermentation softens Zaoyu muscles, but the underlying mechanism remains unknown. Herein, histology and 4D data-independent acquisition (4D-DIA) quantitative proteomics were used to elucidate the texture evolution mechanism of Zaoyu. The hardness and chewiness of Zaoyu decreased early (1-3 days) and increased later (4-6 days), decreasing by 56.09 % and 43.83 % respectively in this nonlinear change. Histology revealed the decrease in hardness was mainly due to the swelling and degradation of muscle fascicles after water absorption, while the increase resulted from severe perimysium destruction and subsequent muscle fascicle aggregation and contraction. Proteomics and bioinformatics identified 34 potential protein markers linked to Zaoyu texture. The degradation of structural proteins and their isoelectric point-induced aggregation explained the fiber degradation and aggregation behaviors. Linker proteins, metabolic enzymes and regulatory factors may promote these behaviors. These findings contribute to understanding the texture change mechanism of fermented fish products and regulate their texture more accurately.
